original text:
Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) has been decimated by the reality that the PC market is shrinking, and while it has made a wonderful move to the Cloud to recapture that lost revenue, now fears surround its ability to deliver the goods with Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) on its heels.
The fear hit a fever pitch just a few month ago when reports came out that Intel was finding its 10nm process node shrink neither easy nor cheap. But now the narrative is gently changing -- we said gently. Perhaps Intel isn't going to be as far behind as expected in the 7nm market, and perhaps buyers will have more patience.
This earnings release will focus on the numbers, as they always do, but the guidance will be the focus. Intel must answer questions about its lag in the 7nm market and when it will be back, if ever, as the technology leader in the space.
Some questions will persist around its GPU product, but for now, that market is so utterly dominated by Nvidia, it's likely a non starter, even if its good news -- in fact, even it's bad news.
One note, Intel's estimates from analysts are so tight, that a guidance number could surprise the market without that much of an upside forecast.
Lastly, Intel is a bellwether, and in that vein, all bulls of this market want to hear about growing demand from the guts of technology -- that's memory and CPUs.
Finally, when we get down to the valuations metrics, we can see, Intel is still forecast to grow, and that leaves its forward fiscal year 2020 PE ratio at about 10.
* If INTC reports a revenue number above $ 18.4 Billion , that would be ahead of even the highest estimates.
* If INTC reports an EPS number above $ 1.18 , that would be ahead of even the highest estimates.
* If INTC guides revenue above $ 18.96 Billion , that would be ahead of even the highest estimates.
* If INTC guides EPS above $ 1.19 , that would be ahead of even the highest estimates.
* If INTC hits the mean estimates for EPS this year, the company trades at a forward PE (for the current fiscal year) of 10.81 .
* If INTC hits the mean estimates for EPS for next year, the company trades at a forward PE (for the next fiscal year) of 10.64 .

Detached 3 bedroom bungalow with space at the back for future development in a serene neighborhood off omololu off randle avenue surulere 25million asking.Title:Certificate of sales. Let deal.

The property can be access through isawo road to Asolo bus stop and Ita-oluwo bus stop via PZ ikorodu Lagos.. Titles:- Purchasing receipt, Family Deed of Assignment. Asking price 6m... N. original text:
@joser916: Have you tried installing the version linked a mere three posts above?
it works hell ya i got my lil toy back!
Something aint right. I updated Firefox 1.5.0.1. Had to make Super D&G compatible. Wheras it worked fine before the Firefox update, I had to diable it cos it kept causing Firefox to freeze which in turn caused everything else to lock up. About 6 manual reeboots later only by disabling this one extension did everything else work OK. Boy, I miss this extension - it was sooooooooo good. Update please!
I want to replace default directional search engines with my favorite ones.
I've changed superdragandgo-0.2.6.xpi to superdragandgo-0.2.6.zip -> unpacked -> 'chrome' folder -> changed superdrag.jar to superdrag.zip -> unpacked -> 'content' folder -> edited superdrag.js -> what is next?
I've tried to create .zip and then change to .jar but seems it doesn't work since when installing I get an error: Install script not found. How to create .jar file?
rozin wrote: I want to replace default directional search engines with my favorite ones.
A .jar file is just a .zip file with a different extension.
1. Did you put the .jar file back in the chrome directory of the .XPI file?
2. Did you accidentally delete the install.rdf and/or chrome.manifest file that should exist in the root of the .XPI file?
You should put things back EXACTLY where you found them. The directory structure is very important.
As I've mentioned in some other thread, (generic) you should use a shell extension or utility like WinZIP or WinRAR that makes .ZIP (and hence .JAR and .XPI) archives look like regular subdirectories. This way you can see where exactly you are drag'n'dropping things. I use Total Commander personally.
foreword: i posted this on the website on the formular, but the text seemed not to be formatted, so i came here and posted it -formatted- again.
-same as above for text.
please tell me, what do you think.
There is a bug dragging links formatted as display:block. See testcase here.
For Super DragAndGo on Mac OS X, Is there a special format in specifying the directory where 'dragged' images are saved? This is when bypassing the 'use download manager' option in the preferences.
I've tried different combinations of filepaths with preceding slashes/drivenames/etc and I'm having no luck.
How do I disable drag to left open in background and right foreground, which it seems to only do to images.
It seems that the searchbox API has been totally reworked in Firefox 2, and as a result, SDAG's drag-to-search feature is broken with Firefox 2. I took 0.2.6 (non-directional) and redid the drag-and-search code so that it works now with Firefox 2's new searchbox implementation.
Can somebody please do as code65536 mentions on that web page (code diffs, etc.) and find a good version of the directional SuperDragandGo with security patch and apply his modifications to make it work with Firefox 2? If someone does so it could probably be hosted on that same site and people would have their choice of if they want directional or not.
P.S. Thanks very much code65536 for fixing this extremely useful extension for us. There was a thread on mozillazine about SuperDragandGo not working with FF2, a while back (a month?) and someone posted a fixed version, based off 2.4 I recall. I don't believe it was you, but I can't find that thread with the search easily right now.
I've switched from Super Drag'n'Go to Drag de Go. It's kept up to date and has far far more configuration and gesture options. You can configure searching gestures, too.
